title: WOS Verification Report
description: A WOS Verification Report sidecar document. Records the results of SMT-based formal verification of deontic constraints and governance rules. Captures which constraints were verified, the result (proven-safe, proven-unsafe, inconclusive), counterexamples, and solver metadata. Reports are immutable provenance artifacts.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosVerificationReport.description: WOS Verification Report specification version. MUST be '1.0'. Identifies this document as a WOS Verification Report provenance artifact and pins the specification version. Processors reject documents with unsupported versions.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Verification Report sidecar

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document whose constraints were verified. Follows the same targetWorkflow binding pattern as Layer 1 and Layer 2 sidecars. Used by tooling to cross-reference this report with the governing advanced governance document.

x-lm.intent: Binds this report to the verified workflow

version.description: Version of this Verification Report document. Typically matches the advanced governance document version that was verified. SemVer is RECOMMENDED.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time when the SMT verification run was performed. Recorded for audit trail purposes; reports are immutable after this timestamp.

examples.notes: Constraint holds for all possible input combinations

x-lm.intent: The verification outcome for each constraint -- proven-safe, proven-unsafe, or inconclusive

name.description: Name of the SMT solver or verification tool that produced these results. Recorded for reproducibility and audit. Standard solvers: Z3, CVC5. Custom verifiers MUST use a unique name.

version.description: Version of the SMT solver or verification tool. Combined with 'name' to form a complete tool identification for audit and reproducibility purposes.

timeout.description: Maximum time the solver is allowed per constraint before recording an 'inconclusive' result. ISO 8601 duration. Balances thoroughness against CI/CD pipeline time budgets.

constraintRef.description: Reference to the deontic constraint id in the AI Integration Document that was subject to SMT verification. Must match a constraintRef declared in the advanced governance verifiableConstraints list (Advanced S8.1).

x-lm.intent: Identifies which constraint this result applies to

result.description: Verification result. 'proven-safe': constraint holds for all possible inputs within declared domains. 'proven-unsafe': a counterexample exists. 'inconclusive': solver timed out or construct outside verifiable subset.

x-lm.intent: The formal verification outcome for this constraint

solverTimeMs.description: Wall-clock time in milliseconds the solver spent on this constraint. Useful for identifying performance bottlenecks and tuning timeout thresholds.

notes.description: Additional notes from the verification process explaining the result, including which verifiable subset restrictions apply or why the result is inconclusive.
examples: Constraint holds for all possible input combinations within declared finite domains.

inputs.description: Mapping of input field paths to values that constitute a counterexample — a concrete assignment that causes the constraint to be violated. Used to reproduce and debug the unsafe case.

explanation.description: Human-readable explanation of why this input assignment violates the constraint. Describes the logical path from the counterexample inputs to the constraint violation.
examples: When output.eligible is false and impactLevel is rights-impacting, the noFinalDenial prohibition is violated because no human review step precedes the denial.

totalConstraints.description: Total number of constraints that were submitted for verification in this run. Equal to the sum of provenSafe, provenUnsafe, and inconclusive.

provenSafe.description: Number of constraints that were formally proven to hold for all possible inputs within the declared finite domains and verifiable subset restrictions.

provenUnsafe.description: Number of constraints for which a counterexample was found. These constraints do NOT hold and MUST be reviewed and addressed before deployment.

inconclusive.description: Number of constraints where the solver timed out or the constraint used constructs outside the verifiable subset. Inconclusive results require manual review.

totalSolverTimeMs.description: Total wall-clock time in milliseconds the solver spent across all constraints in this run. Sum of all per-constraint solverTimeMs values.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
